skill.name: docs
skill.description: Creates Japanese NotebookLM slide customization YAML with two presentation design directions.
skill.summary: Generate paste-ready NotebookLM slide design YAML for documentation and presentation workflows.
skill.author: Chachamaru127

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed in context and all confirmed issues were dismissed as false positives. The files are Markdown instructions and YAML examples, with no executable code, no shell invocation, no cryptographic logic, and no automatic network access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown inline code markers and fenced YAML examples. No Ruby, shell, or command execution behavior is present in the reviewed documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown formatting or YAML example blocks. I found no executable file, command runner, or user-controlled command path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reference URLs Reported as Network Activity
low_findings.description: The network detections are two documentation reference links. The skill does not fetch these URLs or instruct the model to send data to them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are listed under a reference section only. There is no code or instruction that performs automatic network access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Crypto and Entropy Heuristics on Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and high entropy detections occur in Markdown front matter, tags, Japanese text, URL strings, and YAML comments. No cryptographic algorithm, encoded payload, or obfuscated executable content was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files contain prose and static YAML examples only. The entropy signal is explained by mixed-language documentation, URLs, and dense design examples rather than an encoded payload.

content.value_statement: NotebookLM slide customization can be hard to control when design direction is vague. This skill creates two structured Japanese YAML design options for clearer slide generation.
actual_capabilities: Routes documentation requests to NotebookLM YAML generation guidance.
Produces two Japanese design directions for NotebookLM slide customization.
Collects purpose, audience, tone, brand elements, and visual balance.
Defines slide style rules for layout, color, typography, and spacing.
Includes corporate and editorial presentation design patterns.
limitations: It does not create a finished slide deck.
It does not upload content to NotebookLM or use a NotebookLM API.
It is optimized for Japanese slide design instructions.
Output quality depends on the source material and user context.

use_cases.title: Prepare Internal Briefing Slides
use_cases.description: Create a clear corporate design direction for project updates or team reports.

use_cases.title: Package Research for Presentation
use_cases.description: Turn NotebookLM source material into structured visual guidance for a polished deck.

use_cases.title: Explore Two Design Directions
use_cases.description: Compare a minimal data-led style with an editorial story-led style before generating slides.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Slide YAML
prompt_templates.prompt: Create NotebookLM slide customization YAML for an internal update. Use the default questions if I do not provide enough detail.

prompt_templates.title: Customize for Audience
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with audience and tone.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate two Japanese NotebookLM slide design YAML options for executives. The tone should be trusted, concise, and data focused.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Brand Constraints
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with brand and layout constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create two NotebookLM slide YAML directions using our brand color, simple charts, wide margins, and a no-heavy-shadow rule.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Existing Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing slide design brief.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this NotebookLM slide design direction and rewrite it as two stronger YAML options with clearer layout rules and limitations.

output_examples.input: I need NotebookLM slides for a product strategy update.
output: A short set of clarification questions about audience, purpose, and tone.
A minimal corporate direction focused on readability and data.
An editorial direction focused on story, spacing, and visual rhythm.

output_examples.input: Create a slide design prompt for a recruiting presentation.
output: A Japanese design brief for a warm and trustworthy recruiting deck.
Two alternative visual approaches with color, layout, typography, and image guidance.
Clear rules for what to avoid, such as excessive decoration or visual clutter.

best_practices: Provide audience, purpose, tone, brand colors, and source type before generation.
Choose one of the two directions before pasting into NotebookLM.
Review colors and typography against your organization guidelines.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to generate final presentation files.
Do not use it for code review, deployment, or implementation tasks.
Do not paste sensitive internal material unless your AI tool and NotebookLM setup allow it.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates Japanese YAML-style design instructions for NotebookLM slide customization.

faq.question: Does it create actual slides?
faq.answer: No. It creates design guidance that you can paste into NotebookLM.

faq.question: Why does it create two options?
faq.answer: It gives one corporate, data-focused option and one editorial, story-focused option.

faq.question: Can I use it in Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Does it run commands or access the network?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed files are documentation and static YAML examples only.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the presentation purpose, target audience, tone, brand elements, and visual preference.
